response.justification: Candidate is a seasoned full-stack engineer with strong web/backend credentials and some C/C++ exposure at National Instruments (2014-2015, ~6 months), but lacks the modern C++ or Rust commercial depth this role demands. No demonstrated experience with routing/navigation systems, geospatial data pipelines at production scale, or distributed-system primitives like fault tolerance and consistency at the level required. GIS exposure is meaningful but sits on the application/data-collection side, not the routing-tile pipeline side.
strengths: Genuine GIS/geospatial background through GISCollective and Green Map work
Low-latency systems experience at Dunnhumby with async I/O, fibers, and high-throughput pipelines
Polyglot engineer comfortable picking up new stacks and operating across the full technical surface
concerns: No recent modern C++ or Rust commercial experience; primary stack is TypeScript/Node and DLang
No demonstrated work on routing tiles, map data pipelines, or navigation guidance QA
Distributed-systems depth (consistency, fault tolerance, service discovery at global scale) is not evidenced

key_factors.factor: Lack of modern C++/Rust commercial experience
